message:
chore(beads): sync issue tracker state

Update beads issue tracker to reflect current project status.
This synchronizes the local issue database with recent work.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -32,7 +32,7 @@
 {"id":"bd-1h8","title":"EPIC: Harness integration for runtime evidence (asupersync + frankentui)","description":"Goal: make runtime_math and membrane behavior provable and debuggable via deterministic harness outputs.

Requirements (AGENTS.md):
- Use /dp/asupersync for deterministic orchestration + traceability primitives.
- Use /dp/frankentui for deterministic diff/snapshot reporting and TUI analysis.

Outputs:
- Harness commands that capture runtime_math snapshots and evidence ledgers.
- Deterministic diffs for regressions (repair rate, risk bounds, regret, etc.).
- Optional offline decode of erasure-coded evidence (if RaptorQ evidence is enabled).

Non-goal:
- Introducing these crates as runtime libc dependencies.","notes":"Status: all blocking beads are closed (snapshot capture + diff UI + regression report + evidence decode).

Harness surface (tooling-only; no runtime deps):
- `harness snapshot-kernel` (deterministic RuntimeKernelSnapshot fixture)
- `harness diff-kernel-snapshot` (plain + ftui via `frankentui-ui`)
- `harness kernel-regression-report` (strict vs hardened report)
- `harness decode-evidence` (offline evidence decode + deterministic proof/render)

Implementation anchors:
- Snapshots: `crates/glibc-rs-harness/src/kernel_snapshot.rs`
- Diffs/UI: `crates/glibc-rs-harness/src/snapshot_diff.rs` (+ new `evidence_decode_render.rs`)
- Evidence decode: `crates/glibc-rs-harness/src/evidence_decode.rs`

Stability fix:
- `kernel_snapshot` capture now runs on a dedicated thread with explicit stack size to avoid libtest stack overflow when debug-rendering the large `RuntimeKernelSnapshot`.

Quality gates: `cargo fmt/check/clippy/test --all-targets` are green in the current worktree.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"feature","assignee":"IndigoEagle","created_at":"2026-02-09T21:30:15.629931340Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-11T02:26:17.684951624Z","closed_at":"2026-02-11T02:26:17.684924222Z","close_reason":"Harness now provides deterministic snapshot/diff/regression + evidence decode outputs via asupersync/frankentui tooling","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1h8","depends_on_id":"bd-215","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-09T21:35:50.480993365Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-1h8","depends_on_id":"bd-2ds","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-09T21:35:50.402168239Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-1h8","depends_on_id":"bd-3aa","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-09T21:35:50.320562139Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-1h8","depends_on_id":"bd-3v3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-09T21:35:50.242428998Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-1h8","depends_on_id":"bd-5ky","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-09T21:35:50.560385392Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":7,"issue_id":"bd-1h8","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"## Epic Notes: Harness Integration (asupersync + frankentui)

### Why This Exists
Runtime math and membrane healing are only valuable if we can:
- reproduce decisions deterministically
- compare behavior vs host glibc and vs our own previous versions
- explain why a decision changed (evidence ledger)

### Hard Constraint
`/dp/asupersync` and `/dp/frankentui` are **tooling only** for glibc_rust. libc runtime must not link them.

### asupersync Role
- deterministic orchestration of conformance scenarios
- traceability primitives and stable virtual time
- scripted injection of corruption/loss patterns for evidence/FEC tests

### frankentui Role
- snapshot/diff oriented output for:
  - RuntimeKernelSnapshot
  - evidence ledger entries
  - raptorq-style decode proofs

### Artifact Contracts (Make It Mechanical)
- `RuntimeKernelSnapshot` capture format must be stable + versioned.
- Harness must be able to:
  - run scenario -> capture snapshot -> diff vs golden -> emit report
  - decode evidence stream offline -> verify -> emit proof + diff

### Definition Of Done
- One-command harness entrypoint produces deterministic report.
- Snapshot schema changes are deliberate (versioned) and show up in diffs.
- Evidence decode/proof output is readable and debuggable.","created_at":"2026-02-09T21:52:09Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-1hq","title":"Kernel: Groebner normal form for violation signatures (design)","description":"Design Groebner-basis normal-form canonicalization for anomaly/violation signatures.

Goal:
- Produce stable root-cause IDs from many overlapping anomaly signals.
- Avoid double-counting and improve sparse latent-cause recovery.

Design tasks:
- Define signature variables (small integer features derived from cached states).
- Define constraints/relations and what the normal form represents.
- Choose offline artifact: Groebner basis + reduction table (no heavy algebra at runtime).

Acceptance criteria:
- Concrete signature schema + deterministic reduction procedure + output ID format.","notes":"Drafted design doc: crates/glibc-rs-membrane/src/runtime_math/grobner_normal_form_design.md (v1 atom schema A00..A24 + optional class vars C0..C5; relations/term order; offline artifact format; deterministic reduction; id format).","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"BrightCave","created_at":"2026-02-09T21:32:55.351807261Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-10T19:13:26.094075093Z","closed_at":"2026-02-10T19:13:26.094056689Z","close_reason":"Drafted crates/glibc-rs-membrane/src/runtime_math/grobner_normal_form_design.md with v1 schema, relations/term order, offline artifact sketch, deterministic reduction procedure, and gn1 id format.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"bd-1ik","title":"Opportunity matrix workflow: score hotspots and enforce score>=2.0 before implementation","description":"Extreme optimization mapping.

Deliverables:
- Implement hotspot scoring matrix with impact/confidence/effort and computed score.
- Gate optimization work so only score>=2.0 items proceed.

Acceptance:
- Optimization backlog always includes explicit score rationale.
- Low-score work remains deferred unless manually justified.

Evidence & Test Requirements:
- Add comprehensive unit tests (happy-path, negative, adversarial, and regression cases) for all changed logic.
- Add or update deterministic e2e scripts that exercise strict and hardened modes where relevant.
- Emit structured detailed logs for test/e2e runs (trace_id, mode, API/symbol, outcome, errno, timing, and artifact refs).
- Bead cannot close until test commands and log artifact locations are documented.","notes":"2026-02-11 closure (CrimsonCove): opportunity_matrix_test (7 pass) + gate script. Scoring dimensions defined, threshold enforced (>=2.0), scores match formula, entries have required fields.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"CrimsonCove","created_at":"2026-02-11T02:48:12.172285695Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-11T16:52:42.521959Z","closed_at":"2026-02-11T16:52:42.521959Z","close_reason":"Opportunity matrix workflow operational. 7 harness tests pass. check_opportunity_matrix.sh gate validates scores, thresholds, and formula consistency.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["critique","perf","planning"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1ik","depends_on_id":"bd-26o","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-11T18:38:01Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
-{"id":"bd-1iya","title":"bd-rqn subtask: Compile-time profile gates enforcing production-only default kernel","description":"Background:
- Classification alone is insufficient; feature/build boundaries must enforce it.

Goal:
- Implement compile-time gating so default production builds exclude research-only controllers by construction.

Deliverables:
1) Feature flag architecture for production vs research kernels.
2) Build profile checks ensuring expected module set per profile.
3) Guard scripts to detect accidental profile drift.

Acceptance Criteria:
- Default production build contains only approved production controllers.
- Research profile can opt-in additional controllers without affecting production path.

Verification & Logging:
- Unit tests for profile/module mapping.
- CI checks across profile combinations.
- Structured logs for build-profile validation outcomes.","status":"open","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-12T15:02:38.464980345Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-12T15:02:38.464980345Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["build","ci","critique","math","verification"]}
+{"id":"bd-1iya","title":"bd-rqn subtask: Compile-time profile gates enforcing production-only default kernel","description":"Background:
- Classification alone is insufficient; feature/build boundaries must enforce it.

Goal:
- Implement compile-time gating so default production builds exclude research-only controllers by construction.

Deliverables:
1) Feature flag architecture for production vs research kernels.
2) Build profile checks ensuring expected module set per profile.
3) Guard scripts to detect accidental profile drift.

Acceptance Criteria:
- Default production build contains only approved production controllers.
- Research profile can opt-in additional controllers without affecting production path.

Verification & Logging:
- Unit tests for profile/module mapping.
- CI checks across profile combinations.
- Structured logs for build-profile validation outcomes.","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"Codex","created_at":"2026-02-12T15:02:38.464980345Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-12T22:33:43.581040749Z","closed_at":"2026-02-12T22:33:36.904979185Z","close_reason":"Implemented runtime_math profile-gate script + compile-time enforcement + harness mapping tests + CI extended-gate hook","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["build","ci","critique","math","verification"],"comments":[{"id":114,"issue_id":"bd-1iya","author":"Codex","text":"Starting bd-1iya. Plan: (1) define Cargo feature/profile split for production vs research runtime_math modules (default=production), (2) generate/validate compile-time module set vs manifest+classification matrix, (3) add gate script + harness tests to prevent drift, (4) wire into scripts/ci.sh extended gates.","created_at":"2026-02-12T22:24:22Z"},{"id":116,"issue_id":"bd-1iya","author":"Codex","text":"Landed bd-1iya in commit 4d3bd6c.

Changes:
- Enforced  at compile time (membrane compile_error if disabled)
- Added build-profile gate: scripts/check_runtime_math_profile_gates.sh (writes JSONL logs + report)
- Added harness mapping test: crates/frankenlibc-harness/tests/runtime_math_profile_gates_test.rs
- Wired gate into scripts/ci.sh extended gates

Validation:
- scripts/check_runtime_math_profile_gates.sh (PASS)
- cargo test -p frankenlibc-harness --test runtime_math_profile_gates_test (3 passed)
- cargo fmt --check (PASS)

Structured artifacts:
- target/conformance/runtime_math_profile_gates.log.jsonl
- target/conformance/runtime_math_profile_gates.report.json","created_at":"2026-02-12T22:33:43Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-1j2u","title":"bd-1gh subtask: pthread_key registry architecture and lifecycle semantics","description":"Background:
- pthread_key APIs are currently blocking stub-burn-down progress and depend on thread lifecycle correctness.

Goal:
- Define and implement key-table architecture with deterministic key allocation/deallocation semantics.

Deliverables:
1) Global key registry design and limits policy.
2) Thread-local value slot mapping strategy.
3) key_create/key_delete semantics and error paths.

Acceptance Criteria:
- Key lifecycle semantics are deterministic and race-safe.
- Explicit handling for exhausted key space and invalid keys.

Verification & Logging:
- Unit tests for key allocation/deletion and invalid operations.
- Structured logs: trace_id, key_id, op, result, errno, timing.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-12T15:00:52.416477429Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-12T15:00:52.416477429Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["critique","implementation","pthread","testing","tls"]}
 {"id":"bd-1j4","title":"EPIC: Hard Parts Roadmap (rtld/CRT + NSS/locale/resolver)","description":"Critique mapping: #4.

Goal: make the ‘hard parts’ explicit with phased milestones.

Deliverables:
- Bootstrap strategy doc (what ‘drop-in replacement’ means for us).
- Incremental plan for crt startup, loader, NSS/resolv, locale/iconv.

Acceptance:
- Each missing subsystem has a bead with acceptance tests and a measurable milestone.

Verification Mandate:
- Every implementation child bead MUST ship comprehensive unit tests and deterministic e2e scripts (strict + hardened where applicable).
- Every test/e2e execution MUST emit detailed structured logs (trace_id, mode, symbol/API family, decision path, errno/outcome, timing, and artifact pointers).
- No bead may close without: test commands, expected outputs, and failure-log artifact examples documented in bead notes or linked reports.

Evidence & Test Requirements:
- Add comprehensive unit tests (happy-path, negative, adversarial, and regression cases) for all changed logic.
- Add or update deterministic e2e scripts that exercise strict and hardened modes where relevant.
- Emit structured detailed logs for test/e2e runs (trace_id, mode, API/symbol, outcome, errno, timing, and artifact refs).
- Bead cannot close until test commands and log artifact locations are documented.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-02-11T02:36:07.020884278Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-12T15:05:50.562069627Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["critique","locale","nss","resolver","rtld"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1j4","depends_on_id":"bd-144","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-11T05:40:06.335497079Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-1j4","depends_on_id":"bd-1rf","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-11T02:48:53.231831734Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-1j4","depends_on_id":"bd-1y7","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-11T02:48:53.340523866Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-1j4","depends_on_id":"bd-25n","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-11T05:40:06.160720739Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-1j4","depends_on_id":"bd-2bu","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-11T02:48:52.911903423Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-1j4","depends_on_id":"bd-2ez","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-11T05:40:06.248988787Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-1j4","depends_on_id":"bd-2mwc","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-12T15:05:50.451947545Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-1j4","depends_on_id":"bd-3bg","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-11T02:48:53.561829962Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-1j4","depends_on_id":"bd-3pe","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-11T02:48:53.454884679Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-1j4","depends_on_id":"bd-3rn","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-11T02:48:53.131829236Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-1j4","depends_on_id":"bd-3u0","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-11T02:48:53.657754217Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-1j4","depends_on_id":"bd-8sho","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-12T15:05:50.561986482Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-1j4","depends_on_id":"bd-qwm","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-11T02:48:53.027992872Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-1j4","depends_on_id":"bd-y45u","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-12T15:05:50.339905828Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-1j4.1","title":"CRT phase-1 hardening: startup DAG + secure-mode invariants","description":"Background:
- CRT/bootstrap correctness determines whether standalone claims are trustworthy.

Scope:
- Advance startup sequencing from skeleton to validated startup DAG with secure-mode invariants and witness hashes.
- Cover argv/envp/auxv transfer and init-order safety checks.

Deliverables:
1) Startup DAG definition and witness hash format.
2) Secure-mode classification rules with tests.
3) Bootstrap conformance fixtures and failure matrix.

Acceptance Criteria:
- Controlled binaries start through internal bootstrap path for covered cases.
- Init-order and secure-mode invariants are machine-checked.

Testing/Logging:
- Unit tests for DAG/witness generation and secure-mode rules.
- E2E startup fixtures (strict/hardened where relevant).
- Logs: trace_id, startup_stage, secure_mode, invariant_status, witness_ref.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-12T15:04:27.466059864Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-12T15:04:31.151437983Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["crt","security","startup"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1j4.1","depends_on_id":"bd-1j4","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-12T15:04:27.466059864Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-1j4.1","depends_on_id":"bd-qwm","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-12T15:04:31.151374404Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
@@ -163,7 +163,7 @@
 {"id":"bd-33p","title":"Evidence/logging unification v2: schema, joins, and artifact index integrity","description":"Background:
- Existing logging contracts are solid, but closure quality depends on consistent evidence across unit/e2e/perf/release workflows.

Goal:
- Ensure one canonical structured evidence schema is emitted by all verification surfaces and can be machine-joined across artifacts.

Deliverables:
1) Unified schema profile for unit/e2e/perf/release events.
2) Cross-artifact correlation keys (trace_id, bead_id/work-item, mode, api_family/symbol).
3) Artifact index verifier ensuring every failed test points to concrete diagnostics.
4) CI gate that validates schema conformance and required field completeness.

Acceptance Criteria:
- Every failing path in CI emits parseable evidence with linkable artifacts.
- Schema validator catches missing/invalid fields deterministically.
- Downstream reports can aggregate by trace_id without lossy joins.

Test and Logging Requirements:
- Unit tests for schema serialization/validation and index generation.
- E2E tests that intentionally trigger representative failures and verify evidence completeness.
- Mandatory detailed logs for both success and failure paths.

Alien-artifact Explainability Requirements:
- Evidence records must support decision explainability (which controller/gate/action and why), not just pass/fail telemetry.","status":"open","priority":0,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-02-12T14:59:34.435007918Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-12T15:07:41.796698445Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["critique","diagnostics","logging","verification"]}
 {"id":"bd-33p.1","title":"Canonical evidence schema v2 for unit/conformance/e2e/perf/release","description":"Background:
- Evidence currently exists across many surfaces, but closure needs one canonical schema.

Scope:
- Define schema v2 that unifies unit/e2e/conformance/perf/release events.
- Require consistent keys for cross-join (`trace_id`, `mode`, `symbol`, `bead_id`, `artifact_ref`).

Deliverables:
1) Schema profile with required/optional fields and enums.
2) Versioning + backward-compatibility policy.
3) Compliance checklist for all emitters.

Acceptance Criteria:
- Every gate output validates against schema v2.
- Consumers can aggregate without lossy ad-hoc transforms.

Rationale:
- Makes evidence queryable and supports deterministic triage.

Testing/Logging:
- Unit tests for serialization/validation across event types.
- E2E validation pass on sample gate outputs.
- Logs include schema_version and validation status for each event stream.","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"Codex","created_at":"2026-02-12T15:03:12.706070755Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-12T21:23:46.631171699Z","closed_at":"2026-02-12T21:23:46.631149017Z","close_reason":"Delivered structured log evidence schema v2 (stream/gate/span/profile fields) + updated validators/tests","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["evidence","logging","schema"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-33p.1","depends_on_id":"bd-33p","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-12T15:03:12.706070755Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":94,"issue_id":"bd-33p.1","author":"Codex","text":"Starting bd-33p.1 now. First pass is to inventory existing schemas (tests/conformance/log_schema.json, tests/conformance/closure_evidence_schema.json, runtime_math EVR1 record format) and define a v2 canonical schema that can join unit/conformance/e2e/perf/release artifacts under consistent keys (trace_id, bead_id, mode, api_family/symbol, artifact_ref) with validators + failure-injection tests.","created_at":"2026-02-12T21:12:48Z"},{"id":95,"issue_id":"bd-33p.1","author":"Codex","text":"Progress update: implemented log evidence schema v2 (backward-compatible). Changes: bumped tests/conformance/log_schema.json schema_version to 2 and added forward-looking optional fields for stream/gate/span/profile/healing_action/exit_code/duration_ms; updated crates/frankenlibc-harness/src/structured_log.rs to support/serialize/validate these fields; updated scripts/check_structured_logs.sh to require schema_version>=2; updated crates/frankenlibc-harness/tests/structured_log_test.rs to assert schema_version>=2. Local verification: cargo test -p frankenlibc-harness --test structured_log_test (with isolated CARGO_TARGET_DIR) and scripts/check_structured_logs.sh both pass.","created_at":"2026-02-12T21:20:18Z"},{"id":96,"issue_id":"bd-33p.1","author":"Codex","text":"Follow-up: updated JSONL emitters to start using new v2 join fields: scripts/e2e_suite.sh now emits {stream:e2e, gate:e2e_suite} on every log line; scripts/bd35a_evidence_run.sh now emits {stream:e2e, gate:bd35a_evidence_run}. Verified cargo test -p frankenlibc-harness --test e2e_suite_test passes.","created_at":"2026-02-12T21:23:21Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-33p.2","title":"Cross-crate trace propagation + runtime decision explainability fields","description":"Background:
- Even valid events are low-value if they cannot be correlated end-to-end.

Scope:
- Ensure trace and context propagation across ABI boundary, membrane runtime decision law, harness runners, and report generators.
- Add explicit decision explainability fields (controller, gate, action, risk inputs).

Deliverables:
1) Context propagation contract across crates.
2) Explainability field set and semantics.
3) Fallback behavior when context is missing.

Acceptance Criteria:
- A single failing symbol can be traced from entrypoint to final report.
- Decision reason fields are always present for runtime actions.

Rationale:
- Enables postmortem-grade diagnostics without re-running workloads.

Testing/Logging:
- Unit tests for propagation APIs and field defaults.
- E2E failure path test verifying full trace chain.
- Logs: trace_id, span_id, symbol, decision, risk_inputs, controller_id.","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"Codex","created_at":"2026-02-12T15:03:12.817767467Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-12T21:49:51.306787232Z","closed_at":"2026-02-12T21:49:51.306768226Z","close_reason":"Delivered cross-crate trace propagation + runtime decision explainability contract with fallback and validation/report coverage","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["logging","runtime-math","traceability"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-33p.2","depends_on_id":"bd-33p","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-12T15:03:12.817767467Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-33p.2","depends_on_id":"bd-33p.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-12T15:03:14.869647947Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":97,"issue_id":"bd-33p.2","author":"Codex","text":"bd-33p.1 is closed; bd-33p.2 is now unblocked. Log schema v2 is in tests/conformance/log_schema.json (schema_version=2) and Rust support is in crates/frankenlibc-harness/src/structured_log.rs. New optional join fields: stream, gate, span_id/parent_span_id, profile(Fast|Full), healing_action, exit_code, duration_ms. scripts/e2e_suite.sh and scripts/bd35a_evidence_run.sh now emit stream+gate.","created_at":"2026-02-12T21:23:54Z"},{"id":102,"issue_id":"bd-33p.2","author":"Codex","text":"Implemented bd-33p.2 trace/explainability contract across ABI + membrane + harness. Highlights: (1) runtime decision context in crates/frankenlibc-abi/src/runtime_policy.rs with entrypoint scopes, fallback trace context, per-call explainability snapshot (trace_id/span_id/symbol/controller_id/decision_action/risk inputs + policy/risk/evidence seqno); (2) allocator ABI entrypoints now scope decision context (malloc/free/calloc/realloc/posix_memalign/memalign/aligned_alloc) for symbol-level linkage; (3) structured log contract hardened in crates/frankenlibc-harness/src/structured_log.rs with runtime_decision explainability validation (controller_id/decision_action/risk_inputs); (4) report generator extended via DecisionTraceReport in crates/frankenlibc-harness/src/report.rs to detect missing trace-chain links and aggregate explainability coverage; (5) CI gate updated in scripts/check_structured_logs.sh to enforce decision explainability fields when decision is present. Validation run: cargo test -p frankenlibc-membrane runtime_math::evidence::tests:: -- --nocapture; cargo test -p frankenlibc-abi runtime_policy::tests:: -- --nocapture; cargo test -p frankenlibc-harness --test structured_log_test -- --nocapture; cargo test -p frankenlibc-harness report::tests:: -- --nocapture; scripts/check_structured_logs.sh; cargo check -p frankenlibc-abi; cargo check -p frankenlibc-harness.","created_at":"2026-02-12T21:49:51Z"}]}
-{"id":"bd-33p.3","title":"Evidence compliance gate: schema + artifact index + failure-injection tests","description":"Background:
- Closure requires hard failure when telemetry is incomplete.

Scope:
- Implement CI gate that validates schema conformance and artifact index completeness.
- Add negative tests that intentionally omit fields/artifacts to verify deterministic failure messaging.

Deliverables:
1) Evidence compliance CI job.
2) Failure-injection e2e suite for observability regressions.
3) Triage output format with direct artifact pointers.

Acceptance Criteria:
- Any schema violation fails CI with actionable diagnostics.
- Intentional bad cases fail for the expected reason.

Rationale:
- Guards against silent observability degradation over time.

Testing/Logging:
- Unit tests for index completeness checks.
- E2E tests with injected telemetry defects.
- Logs include violation_code, offending_event, expected_fields, remediation_hint.","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"Codex","created_at":"2026-02-12T15:03:12.929519332Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-12T22:16:37.324828568Z","closed_at":"2026-02-12T22:12:41.980216409Z","close_reason":"Implemented evidence compliance CLI gate + failure-injection tests + triage output format + CI script","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["ci","logging","quality-gate"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-33p.3","depends_on_id":"bd-33p","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-12T15:03:12.929519332Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-33p.3","depends_on_id":"bd-33p.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-12T15:03:14.971393469Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":108,"issue_id":"bd-33p.3","author":"Codex","text":"Starting bd-33p.3.

Verified: cargo fmt --check; cargo test -p frankenlibc-harness --test evidence_compliance_test (4 passed).","created_at":"2026-02-12T22:16:37Z"},{"id":115,"issue_id":"bd-33p.3","author":"Codex","text":"## Evidence Compliance Gate Implemented (bd-33p.3)

Delivered
- Evidence bundle validator: `crates/frankenlibc-harness/src/evidence_compliance.rs`
  - Validates JSONL log schema via `structured_log::validate_log_line`
  - Validates `artifact_index.json` schema + sha256 integrity
  - Enforces that failure outcomes include non-empty `artifact_refs` and that refs exist + are indexed
  - Deterministic violation ordering and actionable violation codes + remediation hints
- Harness CLI: `harness evidence-compliance --workspace-root ... --log ... --artifact-index ... [--output ...]`
  - Emits triage JSON with `{violation_code, offending_event, expected_fields, remediation_hint, artifact_pointer, line_number, message}`
- CI gate script: `scripts/check_evidence_compliance.sh` (runs failure-injection integration tests)
- CI wiring (extended gates): `scripts/ci.sh` now runs evidence compliance gate when `FRANKENLIBC_EXTENDED_GATES=1`

Tests / Verification
- `bash scripts/check_evidence_compliance.sh` (PASS)
- `cargo test -p frankenlibc-harness --test evidence_compliance_test -- --nocapture` (4 passed)

Notes
- Agent Mail MCP server is currently overloaded (`health_level=red`), so coordination is via bead comments for now.","created_at":"2026-02-12T22:27:43Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-33xi","title":"Integration: real-world application testing","description":"Final integration testing with real-world applications.

Goal: Validate FrankenLibC works correctly with production software.

Test Applications (Tier 1 - Must Work):
1. bash - Interactive shell, job control
2. coreutils - ls, cat, cp, mv, grep, etc.
3. Python 3 - Interpreter, pip, basic scripts
4. curl - HTTP/HTTPS requests
5. git - Version control operations

Test Applications (Tier 2 - Should Work):
1. nginx - HTTP server, static files
2. PostgreSQL - Database server
3. Redis - Key-value store
4. Node.js - JavaScript runtime
5. Go programs - Static binaries

Test Scenarios:
1. Basic functionality
2. Long-running operation
3. High-load stress test
4. Error condition handling
5. Graceful shutdown

Validation:
- Functional correctness (same output as glibc)
- Performance (within acceptable overhead)
- Stability (no crashes over extended run)
- Memory (no leaks, bounded overhead)

Infrastructure:
- Docker containers for each application
- Automated test scripts
- Output comparison framework
- Performance monitoring

Success Criteria:
- All Tier 1 applications work correctly
- Tier 2 applications work for basic operations
- No crashes or hangs
- Performance within budgets","status":"open","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-12T15:04:25.579927038Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-12T15:04:45.565953948Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["e2e","integration"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-33xi","depends_on_id":"bd-1m5","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-12T15:04:45.457841270Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-33xi","depends_on_id":"bd-2tq","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-12T15:04:45.565908112Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-33xi","depends_on_id":"bd-2vv","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-12T15:04:45.350850022Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-33zg","title":"bd-h5x subtask: dlfcn boundary policy (replace vs explicit fallback) with guard rails","description":"Background:
- dlfcn behavior is hard to replace fully; we need explicit boundary policy that preserves safety and claim honesty.

Goal:
- Define and implement dlfcn callthrough minimization boundary with explicit allowed fallback semantics.

Deliverables:
1) dlfcn surface classification: replace vs explicit fallback.
2) Guard policy enforcing no unapproved fallback paths.
3) Support matrix + docs updates reflecting exact dlfcn status.

Acceptance Criteria:
- dlfcn callthrough behavior is policy-controlled and auditable.
- Unapproved dlfcn fallback triggers gate failure.

Verification & Logging:
- Unit tests for boundary policy decisions.
- E2E tests for representative dlopen/dlsym workflows.
- Structured logs with symbol/path decision traces.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-12T15:03:32.813499095Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-12T15:03:32.813499095Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["callthrough","critique","dlfcn","docs","verification"]}
 {"id":"bd-34w","title":"Verification matrix drift guard: fail when critique beads lack matrix coverage","description":"Deliverables:
- Add deterministic check that compares open critique bead set against matrix rows.
- Missing rows must fail with actionable diagnostics.

Acceptance:
- New critique bead without matrix row is detected immediately.
- Failure output includes exact bead IDs and missing fields.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"CrimsonCove","created_at":"2026-02-11T05:52:33.016831175Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-11T06:38:36.416332Z","closed_at":"2026-02-11T06:38:36.416332Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["ci","critique","testing","verification"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-34w","depends_on_id":"bd-1s7","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-11T06:38:43Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":75,"issue_id":"bd-34w","author":"CrimsonCove","text":"## Deliverables Complete

### 1. CI gate: scripts/check_matrix_drift.sh
4 checks:
- Matrix file exists
- Drift detection (compare open/in_progress critique beads vs matrix rows)
- Entry schema validation (bead_id, title, obligations, coverage, coverage_summary)
- Dashboard consistency (stats match entries)

### 2. Integration tests: crates/glibc-rs-harness/tests/matrix_drift_test.rs
6 tests:
- drift_guard_script_exists_and_executable
- all_open_critique_beads_have_matrix_rows
- matrix_entries_have_valid_schema
- dashboard_total_matches_entries
- dashboard_coverage_stats_consistent
- no_duplicate_bead_entries

### Test commands
cargo test -p glibc-rs-harness --test matrix_drift_test
bash scripts/check_matrix_drift.sh

All 6 tests pass. CI gate passes (0 missing rows, 0 stale). Clippy clean.","created_at":"2026-02-11T06:38:36.416332Z"}]}
